note: LIVE-data manifest for lean-6module-v1.1 freshness gate. Every LIVE field carries >=2 independent sources fetched at as_of. FILED data (10-K/10-Q line items) rides the dated-source claim_ledger discipline. Price sits -27.0% off 52wk high / +11.6% above 52wk low -- neither extreme is within the 3% low/high-hug band, so this is NOT a hug situation (T2 should pass on distance alone); low_high_hug_justified is still declared true with a substantive justification for completeness/audit-trail purposes, since qualitatively the price IS in the lower part of its range and that positioning is fundamentals-justified (guidance cut, order deceleration, China revenue decline), not a stale-data artifact.

live_fields.low_high_hug_justification: Price is -27.0% below 52wk high of $89.77 and +11.6% above 52wk low of $58.75 -- comfortably outside the 3% hug band on both ends, so this is not a low/high-hug edge case. To the extent the price sits in the lower third of its range, this is NOT a stale-data or price-grab error: GEHC's Q1 2026 results (reported ~2026-04-30) showed a real guidance cut (adj EBIT margin, adj EPS, and FCF guidance all reduced), orders decelerated sharply (+10.3% to +1.1% organic), and confirmed China revenue decline (-15% YoY). The lower-range price legitimately reflects this fundamental deterioration. Confirmed by 2 independent sources showing the identical $65.57 value for the same 2026-07-02 close.

live_fields.value: FY2026 guidance (post-Q1 2026, reported ~2026-04-30): organic revenue growth 3.0%-4.0% (reaffirmed); adjusted EBIT margin 15.4%-15.7% (cut from 15.8%-16.1%); adjusted EPS $4.80-$5.00 (cut from $4.95-$5.15); free cash flow ~$1.6B (cut from ~$1.7B). Tariff impact expected lower in 2026 than 2025, with the largest 2026 impact concentrated in Q1. Next earnings (Q2 2026) expected late July/early August 2026 based on typical cadence -- exact date not confirmed this run.

China revenue: FY2025 $2.03B (9.85% of total, down from FY2024 $2.14B / 10.85%) -- a -15% YoY decline, the only major geography shrinking (US +6.39% to 44.5% share; Other Countries steady at 45.7%). Company's 10-K risk factors identify 'developments in the market in China' -- specifically increased local competition and prevalence of volume-based procurement (VBP) -- as an ongoing risk. Management's qualitative Q1-era commentary describes 'improving win rates, a more robust imaging funnel and some tender wins' but this improvement had not yet appeared in the FY2025 full-year revenue print. Independent industry data shows United Imaging (domestic competitor) reached ~18.2% share in hospitals level-2-and-above by August 2025 (#1 domestic high-end brand), with a 31% revenue CAGR 2018-2024, while GPS (GE+Philips+Siemens combined) still holds high-end-modality share leadership (68%/62%/92% in MR/CT/DSA as of 2024).

Real, active AI-enabled product pipeline as of Q1 2026: Photonova Spectra2 (AI-powered photon-counting CT on proprietary Deep Silicon detector), MIM Software oncology AI tools (LesionID Pro automated lesion segmentation, MIMNeuro PET quantification), Intelerad $2.3B cash acquisition (cloud/AI enterprise imaging, closing/closed in the Q1 2026 window per the cash-flow impact), 'View' next-gen diagnostic viewer (FDA 510(k) cleared Q1 2026), Clarify DL deep-learning reconstruction (pending clearance). Segment reorg combining Imaging+AVS into a new $14.6B 'Advanced Imaging Solutions' unit (Phil Rackliffe named segment President/CEO) is structurally consistent with an AI/software-first integration strategy. HOWEVER: zero sources found this run quantify AI-attributable revenue, ASP premium, or attach rate anywhere in the business -- the strategic narrative is well-evidenced as ACTIVITY but not yet as MONETIZATION.
